title: Artemis II crew splashes down in Pacific Ocean, ending record-breaking moon flyby
summary: The four astronauts touched down on Earth off the coast of California, concluding historic 10-day mission Artemis II splashdown – live updatesThe Artemis II, and the four astronauts aboard the Orion space capsule, has splashed down into the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego, Nasa has confirmed.The spacecraft touched down at 5.07pm (1.07am BST) making the journey around the moon and back officially 9 days 1hr and 32min. The Orion spacecraft traveled 694,481 miles (1,117,659km), Nasa said.
